$ flint --help

flint v1.4.2 — fast linter for shell scripts

USAGE
  flint <command> [options] [file...]

COMMANDS
  check     Lint one or more scripts
  fix       Auto-fix safe issues in place
  init      Create a config file
  hook      Manage git hooks
  lsp       Start the language server

Run `flint <command> --help` for command-specific flags.
EXAMPLES
  Lint a single script and show all warnings:
    flint check deploy.sh --verbose
  Lint every shell script in the repo, excluding generated files:
    flint check src/ --ext sh,bash --exclude src/generated/
  Auto-fix safe issues in a script (creates a .bak before modifying):
    flint fix deploy.sh --backup
  Generate a config with the strict profile as a starting point:
    flint init --config ~/.config/flint/flint.toml --profile strict
  Use a project-local config instead of the user config:
    flint check src/ --config .flint.toml
  Print only errors, no warnings:
    flint check src/ --min-severity error
  Output results as JSON (useful in CI pipelines):
    flint check src/ --format json > /tmp/flint-report.json
  Check a script piped from stdin:
    cat deploy.sh | flint check -
  Run flint as a pre-commit hook for all staged .sh files:
    flint hook install --hook pre-commit --ext sh
  Start the LSP server on a custom socket:
    flint lsp --socket /tmp/flint.sock

CONFIGURATION

  flint looks for config in this order:
    $FLINT_CONFIG  →  ~/.config/flint/flint.toml  →  /etc/flint/defaults.toml

  Full rule reference:    https://docs.flint.sh/rules
  Config file options:    https://docs.flint.sh/config
  Editor integration:     https://docs.flint.sh/editor-integration
